school_name_en: University of Oxford, Saïd Business School
program_name_zh: 金融经济学理学硕士（MFE，本人翻译，非官方译名）
campus: Oxford, UK

requirements.undergrad_background: First class or strong upper second-class undergraduate degree (with honours), or international equivalent, in any discipline; quantitative skills must be very strong. If the degree result is lower, success is very unlikely.
requirements.gpa_requirement: No numeric GPA stated; described as 'first class or strong upper second-class undergraduate degree (with honours), or the international equivalent'. Applicants are referred to the University's general International Qualifications page for equivalence guidance; no China-specific list/GPA published.

toefl.subscores: Listening 22, Reading 24, Writing 24, Speaking 25

requirements.gmat_gre: GMAT or GRE (General) required; no minimum score, no waivers given. 2024 class averages: GMAT (10th Edition) 750, GRE Quant 170, GMAT Focus Edition 698. Scores must be under 5 years old at programme start.
requirements.prerequisites: Working knowledge of calculus, basics of vectors/matrices, probability, statistics and econometrics expected at course start. Degree does not have to be in Finance/Economics.
requirements.work_experience: Not mandatory; preferred if relevant (full-time or internship). One-page CV required.

deadlines.notes: After Stage 3, rolling admissions continue for new candidates until 31 July 2026; decisions typically within ~2 weeks of a complete application. Only Stage 1/2 offer holders are eligible for University/college scholarships. Kira Talent video assessment and 3 recommendation letters due 1 week after chosen application deadline. Application fee £75.

materials: Essay (max 500 words): motivation and 5-year career plan
notes: Data reflects the 2026 entry cycle (start 21 September 2026), confirmed as 'still open' on the official page at time of access (July 2026), with rolling admission until 31 July 2026. Deposit on offer: £9,440. 88 places; average ~1,063 applications/year (2021-24). Applicants must not use AI to write the admissions essay. AI translation of program name; no official Chinese name found on .ac.uk sources.
